rulings.Case Title: BIANCA KING VS. GOBBLE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 14, 2025, Line 1, COMPLIANCE HEARING. Ordered off calendar in light of the prior compliance statement. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. GURGEN ABRAHAMYANTS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Enforce Settlement (C.C.P. Section 664.6) Points And Authorities; Declaration; Order/Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 14, 2025, Line 2, PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion To Enforce Settlement (C.C.P. Section 664.6). The unopposed motion by plaintiff Bank of America, N.A. to enforce a settlement agreement as a judgment is granted. Bank of America shows that the parties agreed to settlement and agreed that the court could enforce it as a judgment pursuant to CCP 664.6. Defendant Gurgen Abrahamyants has defaulted on the settlement agreement. Bank of America is directed to submit a proposed judgment in the requested amount of $7,115.89. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: JINSUN KANG-PERIS VS. KAISER PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 14, 2025, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JINSUN KANG-PERIS' MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Plaintiff Jinsun Kang-Peris's motion for order quashing a subpoena directed to her new employer is granted in part. Her request for sanctions is denied. Kang-Peris concedes that mitigation evidence (records of her earnings and dates of employment) is discoverable. Defendant Kaiser Permanente Medical Group seeks documents related to her hiring, personnel records, discipline or investigation records, and other categories. Kaiser fails to articulate specific facts showing good cause for the discovery Kang-Peris has brought suit against Kaiser Permanente, her former employer. Kaiser seeks through subpoena information from Kang-Peris's new employer: hiring documents, personnel records, payroll records, and other classes of records. Kang-Peris concedes the discoverability of mitigation evidence, i.e., records of her earnings and dates of employment, but asserts relevance and privacy objections to the balance of categories. The court concludes that Kaiser has not articulated a sufficient and nonspeculative basis to discover the information. The motion is granted as to all of the categories specified in the subpoena except records of earnings and dates of employment. Kang-Peris also seeks sanctions. The court finds Kaiser's opposition was supported by substantial justification and does not award sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion By Non-Cookies Defendants To Extend Stay Pending Ruling By The Court On The Petition To Confirm The Arbitration Final Award, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 14, 2025, Line 6, DEFENDANT COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, LLC, COOKIES SF, LLC,, COOKIES CREATIVE PT, LLC,, COOKIES CREATIVE PRODUCTIONS & CONSULTING, INC.,, BIGGERBIZZ, LLC,, LEMONNADE, INC.,, PARKER BERLING, IAN HABENICHT, GILBERT MILAM JR., CRYSTAL MILLICAN, LESJAI CHANG's Motion By Non-Cookies Defendants To Extend Stay Pending Ruling By The Court On The Petition To Confirm The Arbitration Final Award. 2-Notice And Motion By Non-Cookies Defendants To Extend Stay Pending Ruling By The Court On The Petition To Confirm The Arbitration Final Award, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof Moving Party: COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, COOKIES SF, LLC,, COOKIES CREATIVE PT, LLC,, COOKIES CREATIVE PRODUCTIONS & CONSULTING, INC.,, BIGGERBIZZ, LLC,, Filed: Jun 12, 2025 Tentative Ruling: The unopposed motion to extend the stay is denied as moot. The non-Cookies parties seek to extend the stay, according to the notice of motion, "pending the Court's rulings on the Petition to Confirm Final Arbitration Award and Enter Judgment Thereon by Petitioner/Defendant Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc." By companion order today, the court confirms the arbitration award, and accordingly there is no further basis for stay. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon By Petitioner/Defendant Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 14, 2025, Line , DEFENDANT COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, INC.'s Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon By Petitioner/Defendant Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc. 1-Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon By Petitioner/Defendant Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, Inc. Moving Party: COOKIES CREATIVE CONSULTING & PROMOTIONS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Filed: Jun 12, 2025 Tentative Ruling: Cookies Creative Consulting & Promotions, LLC's ("Cookies") motion to confirm arbitration award is granted. As explained in the companion motion to vacate arbitration award, there is no basis to vacate the award. Cookies is therefore entitled to confirmation. (CCP 1286.)
